title: ochna obtusata var. pumila
intro: Ochna obtusata var. pumila, also known as the dwarf mistletoe or Ochna pumila, is a plant species belonging to the family Ochnaceae. It is a shrub-like plant native to tropical Africa, and its leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to leverage its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
